element_introduction_order.element: Nitro boost pickups (6 initial boosts per race)
element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Strategic timing and resource management introduced early
Economy system and upgrade motivation established
Increases complexity; requires damage awareness and defensive driving
Adds tactical layer; tire management becomes critical

element_introduction_order.first_appears: Mid to late game (mandatory by later races)
element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Extended track lengths force resource planning; gearbox becomes mandatory upgrade

element_introduction_order.element: Complex car upgrades (engine, nitro, armor, gearbox, tires, paint)
element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Build customization and strategic upgrade sequencing
Faster, tougher competitors; increases challenge systematically
Stakes and tension; failure excludes progression

typical_relief_triggers: Country completion and password save system
Nitro boost activation for speed surges
typical_tension_triggers: Armor damage accumulation and spinout risk
Opponent AI pressure in later countries
Weather condition changes affecting tire grip
Complex turn navigation at high speeds
Long races requiring fuel economy management
rhythm_pattern.pacing_descriptor: Sustained tension with periodic relief; early game arcade-paced, late game resource-management intensive. Progressive difficulty curve across 16-country campaign maintains engagement through mechanical deepening rather than pure speed escalation.

spatial_grammar.optional_content_signal: Pickups scattered along race routes ($, N for nitro, S for automatic nitro); optional upgrade visits between races

difficulty_curve.early_game: Australasia introduces foundational mechanics with forgiving AI; arcade-style controls and abundant resources (6 nitro boosts initial)
difficulty_curve.mid_game: Weather conditions and damage system complexity increase; opponents become faster and tougher; fuel economy begins limiting car performance on longer tracks
difficulty_curve.late_game: United States and preceding countries demand multiple mandatory upgrades (especially gearbox for fuel economy); longer track lengths force strategic fuel planning; opponent AI significantly more competitive
notable_spikes: Introduction of weather effects (tire change requirement)
Transition to longer races requiring gearbox upgrades
Final countries with extended track layouts and high-speed opponent AI

notable_level_patterns.description: 16 countries × 4 races creates macro-rhythm: each country block introduces regional variance, allows upgrading between races, culminates in country unlock with password save. Password system provides psychological relief point.
notable_level_patterns.example_level: Australasia (races 1-4) establishes pattern; Munich track demonstrates mid-game complexity

notable_level_patterns.description: Top-ten finish requirement creates persistent tension throughout each race. Failure prevents progression, forcing replays and strategy refinement.
notable_level_patterns.example_level: All 64 races implement this mechanic uniformly

notable_level_patterns.description: After each country, player returns to upgrade menu. Gearbox upgrades become mandatory by mid-game to manage fuel on extended tracks. Creates decision tension and strategic planning.
notable_level_patterns.example_level: Transition from Australasia to subsequent countries shows escalating upgrade necessity
